vocab.meaning: chi phí trên mỗi lần sử dụng
vocab.example: I justify spending more on high-quality leather boots because their cost-per-wear is actually lower than cheap disposable fashion.

vocab.meaning: hỗ trợ chỉnh hình/nâng đỡ bàn chân
vocab.example: I am willing to invest a significant portion of my budget in shoes that offer excellent orthopedic support to prevent long-term back pain.

vocab.meaning: đợt mở bán phiên bản giới hạn
vocab.example: Occasionally, I might splurge on a limited-edition drop if the design is particularly unique and holds its resale value.

vocab.meaning: tư duy tiết kiệm/chi tiêu chừng mực
vocab.example: Maintaining a frugal mindset, I typically wait for end-of-season clearances before purchasing any new footwear.
I tend to avoid shoes made of cheap synthetic materials, as they lack breathability and degrade much faster than natural hide.

vocab.meaning: món đồ cơ bản và linh hoạt trong tủ đồ
vocab.example: I spent a fair amount on white sneakers recently because they are a versatile wardrobe staple that pairs with almost any outfit.
Even if a pair of shoes is aesthetically pleasing, I refuse to pay an exorbitant price tag just for a designer logo.
Collector-grade sneakers can be a good investment as their resale value often appreciates significantly over time.

vocab.meaning: may đo riêng biệt (đóng giày thủ công theo số đo)
vocab.example: While I currently buy mass-produced shoes, I hope to one day afford the luxury of bespoke tailoring for a perfect fit.
I usually set aside a small fund for seasonal clearances, which is when I can snag high-end brands at a fraction of their original cost.
